issues.title: [UX-01][P0] Management Dashboard IA: posture, attention, activity, detail bands
issues.body:
## Metadata
| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Sprint** | Enterprise Management Dashboard UX |
| **Priority** | P0 |
| **Type** | UX / Information architecture |
| **Component** | `client/src/components/ManagementDashboard.jsx` |

## Summary
Restructure the Management Dashboard into clear vertical **bands**: (1) executive posture + few summary metrics, (2) **items requiring attention** (consolidated or tabbed), (3) portfolio activity (framework bars + heat map), (4) registers + intelligence with **progressive disclosure** (accordions / collapsed by default on narrow viewports).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Visual order matches: summary → attention → activity → detail.
- [ ] Lower-priority intelligence blocks are collapsible or de-emphasized without losing navigation targets.
- [ ] No change to API contracts or `onNavigate` behaviour.

## Test plan

### Automated
- `cd client && npm run build` — zero compile errors.

### Functional
1. Load Management Dashboard with data; confirm each band is visually distinct (heading or spacing).
2. Resize to 768px width: accordions/collapsed sections still expose primary actions (links to modules).
3. Tab through the page: focus order follows band order; no focus traps inside accordions.
4. Change OpCo and period: all bands still render; no layout collapse.

### Regression
- Every existing drill-through (KPI, heat map cell, expiry row, dependency, data compliance) still navigates to the correct view with context.

### Sign-off
- Product/UX review: "attention" band is identifiable within 5 seconds for a new user.

issues.title: [UX-02][P1] Enterprise typography for Management Dashboard
issues.body:
## Metadata
| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Sprint** | Enterprise Management Dashboard UX |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Type** | UX / Typography |
| **Component** | `client/index.html`, `ManagementDashboard.css`, optional scoped import |

## Summary
Introduce or scope **enterprise-grade** sans (e.g. Source Sans 3, IBM Plex Sans) for `.mgmt-dash` only (or product-wide if approved). Apply **tabular numerals** (`font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`) to KPI values and tables. Reduce **font-weight: 800** display usage to 600–700 for headline numbers.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `.mgmt-dash` uses the chosen stack; fallback to system UI fonts if font fails to load.
- [ ] Metric columns align visually when numbers change (tabular figures).
- [ ] No CLS > 0.1 on dashboard load from font swap (use `font-display: swap` and size-adjust if needed).

## Test plan

### Automated
- `cd client && npm run build`.

### Visual
1. Compare KPI row digits before/after: widths should not jump when counts update (refresh).
2. macOS Safari + Chrome: font loads; no FOUT breaking layout.

### Accessibility
1. Zoom 200%: text remains readable; no clipping in KPI tiles.
2. Windows **High Contrast** mode: metrics and labels remain distinguishable.

### Cross-browser
- Chrome, Firefox, Safari (latest): dashboard renders; monospace only where specified (e.g. timestamps).

### Performance
- Lighthouse performance: no regression > 5 points on Management Dashboard route vs baseline (manual run).

issues.title: [UX-03][P1] KPI tiles: replace emoji with SVG; semantic color only
issues.body:
## Metadata
| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Sprint** | Enterprise Management Dashboard UX |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Type** | UX / Component |
| **Component** | `ManagementDashboard.jsx`, `KpiTile` |

## Summary
Remove **emoji** from KPI tiles. Use **small stroke SVG icons** (monochrome) or text-only. Remove **rainbow** `accentColor` / `kpiAccentClass` map; use neutral cards plus **semantic** border or badge only when state is risk-critical.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Zero emoji characters in Management Dashboard render output.
- [ ] No more than **two** non-neutral semantic hues in the KPI band (e.g. danger + warning), plus brand accent for links.
- [ ] Hover/focus states remain visible.

## Test plan

### Automated
- Search `ManagementDashboard.jsx` for emoji usage; none in user-visible strings.
- `npm run build`.

### Visual
1. All KPI tiles share the same base chrome; only risk tiles show accent.
2. Icons 16–20px, 1.5px stroke, aligned to label baseline.

### Accessibility
1. Icons have `aria-hidden="true"` if decorative; tile `aria-label` or visible label sufficient.
2. Color is not the only differentiator: critical tiles include text or icon shape difference.

### Regression
- Click each tile type: navigation unchanged.

issues.title: [UX-04][P1] ManagementDashboard.css enterprise visual system
issues.body:
## Metadata
| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Sprint** | Enterprise Management Dashboard UX |
| **Priority** | P1 |
| **Type** | UX / CSS |
| **Component** | `ManagementDashboard.css` |

## Summary
Scoped tokens under `.mgmt-dash` (`--mgmt-*`): neutral surfaces, single border style, **subtle** hover (border/background only). Remove `translateY` lift and heavy tinted shadows. **Desaturate** heat map and framework bar fills. Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` to disable non-essential transitions.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] No `transform: translateY` on KPI hover.
- [ ] Heat map cells readable in both light theme and with slight desaturation.
- [ ] Reduced-motion: no animated gauge stroke if it is decorative (or instant).

## Test plan

### Automated
- `npm run build`.
- Confirm no `translateY` in `ManagementDashboard.css` for `.mgmt-kpi-tile:hover`.

### Visual
1. Side-by-side screenshot before/after at 1440px.
2. Heat map: legend or caption still explains intensity.

### Accessibility
1. `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` enabled in OS: verify transitions disabled or minimal.
2. Focus ring 2px visible on all interactive tiles and panel links.

### Performance
- Optional `content-visibility` on off-screen bands if implemented.

issues.title: [UX-05][P2] Attention queues: tables and scannable lists
issues.body:
## Metadata
| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Sprint** | Enterprise Management Dashboard UX |
| **Priority** | P2 |
| **Type** | UX / Content design |
| **Component** | Expiry table, health drivers, dependency top clusters, intel lists |

## Summary
Refactor dense chip stacks into **table or definition-list** patterns where appropriate: columns for **Item**, **Entity/OpCo**, **Severity or due**, **Action** (link). Keep one-line secondary detail; truncate with tooltip for long framework names.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Expiry table has aligned columns; header row styled consistently.
- [ ] "Why this score" drivers are scannable in <=3 seconds per row.
- [ ] Mobile: horizontal scroll OR stacked cards with same field order.

## Test plan

### Automated
- `npm run build`.
- Table uses `th scope="col"` where applicable.

### Functional
1. Stable order: expiring soonest first (if not already).
2. Long OpCo / framework names: ellipsis + `title` tooltip.

### Accessibility
1. Screen reader: table headers announced when navigating cells.
2. Keyboard: row actions reachable without mouse.

### Responsive
- 320px min width sanity: table wrapper scrolls horizontally if needed.

### Content
- Empty states: single sentence + suggested action (e.g. change period).

issues.title: [UX-06][P0] QA gate: accessibility, build, regression checklist
issues.body:
## Metadata
| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Sprint** | Enterprise Management Dashboard UX |
| **Priority** | P0 |
| **Type** | QA |
| **Component** | Management Dashboard (release gate) |

## Summary
Formal **release checklist** after UX-01–UX-05 land. Blocks merge to main if failed items are not waived.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Checklist attached in PR description with all items checked or waived with owner.
- [ ] No open **critical** a11y violations on this view.

## Test plan (exhaustive checklist)

### Build and lint
- [ ] `cd client && npm run build`
- [ ] `cd server && npm test` (ensure no accidental API drift)

### Keyboard
- [ ] Tab order: header controls to KPIs to panels to footer links
- [ ] Enter/Space activates tiles and accordion headers
- [ ] Escape closes any new modal/popover if introduced
- [ ] Visible focus on every interactive element

### Screen reader (VoiceOver or NVDA sample pass)
- [ ] Page has logical landmark (`main` or region) for dashboard content
- [ ] KPI values read with labels (not numbers alone)
- [ ] Status of health score communicated (text, not color alone)

### Visual / contrast
- [ ] Body text vs background meets WCAG AA (4.5:1)
- [ ] Muted secondary text meets 4.5:1 on surface
- [ ] Critical/warning badges meet contrast on their backgrounds

### Motion
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion` respected globally on this page

### Data scenarios
- [ ] Empty portfolio (no changes): empty states render; no broken grids
- [ ] Single OpCo selected: layout holds; banners if any remain correct
- [ ] Long framework names: no layout explosion

### Cross-browser smoke
- [ ] Chrome, Safari, Firefox latest

### Regression
- [ ] All drill-throughs from Dashboard–Module Consistency sprint still work (context preserved)

### Sign-off
- [ ] UX stakeholder approval recorded in PR or issue comment
